one of the Chklra Tithis, or Visti Karanaz or one of the Sthira
1. You know that it is Amavasya — New Moon — when both the lumi-
naries are together. As the Moon goes away from the Sun, the lunar days or
Tithis (i.e. phases) increase at the rate of 12 degrees until the Moon's
distance is 180° when it is called Full Moon. Then the dark fortnight
begins and the Tithis are calculated in the same manner until the distance
between the two luminaries becomes nil again. Hence to get the Tithi
what you have to do is to subtract the Sun's longitude from that of Ihc
Moon. Let us take an example : Let the Sun's position be 8s-3°-29'-37'i'
and the Moon's 3s-20°-26'-20". By subtracting the former from the latter
we get 7s-16°-56'-43" By reducing it to degrees we get 227 roughly. As
